content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (from Topic A's ICM-based autonomous agents) can be repurposed to prioritize which new experiences an active learning agent stores in its long-term memory, solving the 2024-2025 SOTA challenge of scalable memory retention by only keeping high-uncertainty, information-dense samples that improve future task performance. This creates a 'curiosity-gated memory architecture' that automatically prunes low-value redundant data without hand-engineered retention rules.
